scope: Public data and code for the four Ringneck doves used by Deetjen et al.; qualification of one synchronized flight for prescribed-motion external-force validation

article.title: Small deviations in kinematics and body form dictate muscle performances in the finely tuned avian downstroke

selectedBenchmark.selectionReason: smallest compressed SurfFits.mat among the 20 synchronized analysis flights; retains kinematics, processed structured-light surface fits, two measured aerodynamic-force components, and the authors' derived analysis product

requiredArchiveMembers.role: four-bird final body-mass vector used by the authors
same-bird dissection measurements used in the paper
manually measured extended-wing and tail areas
body-motion series used for the authors' inertial-power calculation
processed structured-light surface fits for the selected flight
authors' synchronized derived blade-element, force, inertial, and muscle analysis product

directlyUsableExperimentalTargets: time-resolved horizontal aerodynamic force FxWings after the authors' force-platform processing
time-resolved vertical aerodynamic force FzWings after the authors' force-platform processing
multi-view tracked and triangulated point kinematics
processed structured-light body, tail, and left-wing surface fits
processedOrAssumed: force-platform channels are filtered, baseline-corrected, and synchronized by the source code
tracked points and reconstructed surfaces are smoothed
the right wing is generated by an explicit bilateral-symmetry assumption
lateral aerodynamic force is computed rather than measured
the authors' AeroF1S_w splits total force between wings and includes modeled directions
the final BodyWeight.mat values average flight-platform and dissection estimates
notMeasuredForSelectedBird: bilateral distributed wing mass and inertia
whole-bird center of mass and principal inertia in the replay frame
raw high-speed images, original camera calibration, and original unprocessed 3D reconstructions
measurementBoundary.massModelProvenance: The paper models each wing with 20 point masses and scales a distribution assembled from Berg and Rayner 1995 blackbird data plus other bird measurements; it is not a same-specimen measured inertia tensor.
measurementBoundary.forceTruthRule: Only FxWings and FzWings may be presented as measured external-force targets. Lateral force, per-wing force, blade-element load location, inertial power, torque, and muscle force remain derived quantities unless independently re-established.

remainingBeforeFirstCFDComparison: selectively acquire and CRC-verify the selected flight
decode SurfFits.mat and preserve its coordinate transforms without substituting procedural geometry
independently reconstruct the 1000 Hz kinematics to 2000 Hz force synchronization and sign conventions
derive the experimental repeatability and force-platform uncertainty from the five OB flights before setting quantitative CFD tolerances
declare air properties and domain/far-field treatment with source or uncertainty provenance
run time-step and 8/12/16 spatial refinement before quantitative comparison
validationDisposition.eligibleClaimAfterPassing: prescribed-motion CFD reproduces the measured horizontal and vertical external-force history for a selected real dove flight within predeclared experimental and numerical uncertainty
validationDisposition.schema2Blocker: same-specimen bilateral wing inertia and registered whole-bird inertia remain unmeasured
validationDisposition.hybridRule: Any constructed inertia must carry source distributions and uncertainty and must be labeled hybrid sensitivity input; it cannot satisfy the measured schema-2 release gate.
validationDisposition.nextAction: acquire the 15 MB engineering subset, verify variables and synchronization, then acquire only the selected 656 MB compressed SurfFits member and implement its importer
